steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage states that Reichert's team found corals may store up to 20 million kilograms of microplastics annually in their skeletons and tissues. This specific claim about coral actively storing large quantities of microplastics is the main idea.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice D captures the study's central finding: corals store large amounts of microplastics from ocean waters.
  • A focuses on reef-building (not the study's focus)
  • B introduces vague unresolved questions (not in the text)
  • C only defines microplastics (background detail, not the main idea)
  • D restates the key finding about corals storing microplastics
That matches choice D — D is correct.
